questions.questionText: During your inspection have you noticed changes in the original scope of the project and if yes are those changes without justification.
questions.ewsAlert: Frequent Changes in the scope of the project without justification

questions.questionText: During your inspection have you noticed as to whether the propsed project cost was significantly higher than actual workn done or the industry standard.
questions.ewsAlert: Project cost significantly higher than industry standard

questions.questionText: During your inspection have you noticed any recent resignation of key personnel or any frequent changes in the top management.
questions.ewsAlert: Resignation of key personnel or frequent Top management changes

questions.questionText: During the course of your inspection have you noticed any case of undue delay in the implementaion of the proeject against the original sanctioned plan.
questions.ewsAlert: Under delay in project implementation vs plan.

questions.questionText: During your inspection through discreet enquiries made by you has the news of loss of any major customer come across your attention.
questions.ewsAlert: Loss of a major customer of the borrower

questions.questionText: During the course of your inspection have you observed any decline in the plant's capacity utilization over projections or past utilization levels.
questions.ewsAlert: Decline in capacity utilization of the borrower's plant/facility

questions.questionText: Whether the inventory audited is overestimated or underestimated?
Are the invoices verified devoid of TAN, GSTIN or other important details?
Do the stocks verified have any disputes on their title of ownership?
Has related party stock movement been observed during the course of your verification?
Has the borrower approached you with an unjustified request for delay of inspection?
Have you faced any resistance/unwillingness from the borrower during the period of audit?
Have you received any request from the borrower asking you to delay your audit?
Have you observed any instance of the same stock being pledged to multiple banks?
Have you observed any instance of the borrower concealing important documentation relevant to your audit?
Have you observed any other bank’s board/nameplate at the site of your audit?
Have you observed instances of inadequate security at the inventory location?
Have you observed any damaged, idle or obsolete stock submitted as part of drawing power calculations?
Have you observed any mismatch in physical register and physical stock?
Default in payment to the bank, sundry debtors, and statutory bodies.
Raid by Income Tax / Sales Tax / Central Excise officials observed.
Inventory appears underinsured or overinsured.
Significant inventory movement disproportionate to turnover observed.
Non-submission of original bills observed.
Significant debtors outstanding beyond 180 days observed.
Increasing trend of debtors outstanding beyond 180 days observed.

rules.description: Last inspection visit was conducted over 1 year ago.

from.table: (SELECT account_number, MAX(createdatetime) AS inspection_date FROM Agriculture_inspection GROUP BY account_number UNION ALL SELECT account_number, MAX(date_of_visit) AS inspection_date FROM MsmeInspection GROUP BY account_number UNION ALL SELECT account_number, MAX(createdatetime) AS inspection_date FROM PsegementInspection GROUP BY account_number)

from.table: (SELECT account_number, MAX(createdatetime) AS inspection_date FROM Agriculture_inspection GROUP BY account_number UNION ALL SELECT account_number, MAX(date_of_visit) AS inspection_date FROM MsmeInspection GROUP BY account_number UNION ALL SELECT account_number, MAX(createdatetime) AS inspection_date FROM PsegementInspection GROUP BY account_number)

rules.description: Not routing of sales proceeds through bank

select.remarks: CONCAT('Credits are low for account ', c.acct_no)

joins.on: c.acct_no = t.to_ac AND t.tran_ts >= DATE_SUB(:runDate, INTERVAL :lookbackDays DAY)

children.left: SUM(CASE WHEN t.amount > 0 THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) - (:thresholdFactor * ((MAX(c.loan_limit) * 5) / 4))
SUM(CASE WHEN t.amount > 0 THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END)

versions.notes: Imported from legacy alertId=13 as native SQL DSL

rules.description: Borrower has multiple gold loan accounts beyond threshold.

select.remarks: CONCAT('Multiple gold loans for customer ', c.cust_number)

versions.notes: Imported from legacy alertId=143 as native SQL DSL

rules.description: Staff account credits/debits to own linked account above threshold.

select.remarks: CONCAT('Staff CIF transfer to own account on ', DATE(t1.tran_ts))

joins.on: s1.customer_id = s2.customer_id AND s1.ac_category = 'STAFF' AND s2.ac_category = 'INDIVIDUALS'
t2.to_ac = s2.account_no AND t1.amount = t2.amount AND t1.tran_ts = t2.tran_ts

rules.description: NPA accounts closed by sanction of fresh loans to the same customer under the same CIF on the same date (from FRM)

select.remarks: CONCAT('Closed NPA account ', c1.acct_no, ' reopened on same day under CIF ', c1.cust_number)

joins.on: c1.cust_number = c2.cust_number AND c1.closing_date = c2.sanct_dt AND c1.acct_no != c2.acct_no

definitions.label: Authority Code
definitions.description: Authority/type code used by specific legacy-migrated rules.

definitions.label: Threshold Count
definitions.description: Count threshold used by legacy-migrated SQL rules.

definitions.label: Threshold Factor
definitions.description: Multiplier or ratio factor used by legacy-migrated SQL rules.

definitions.label: Threshold Percentage
definitions.description: Percentage threshold used by legacy-migrated rules.

definitions.label: Threshold Value
definitions.description: Primary threshold value used by legacy-migrated rules.

definitions.label: Window Days
definitions.description: Window size in days for time-bound checks.
